document_decision.reason: 论文提供三种催化剂热分解气氛、统一甲烷 CCVD 条件以及对应产率、TGA 碳含量和 CNT 结构证据，可形成三个对比 run。
warnings: 仅报告 900 °C 生长条件，不能据此建立温度窗口或作者最优温度。
Table 1 的 purity/carbon yield 来自 TGA 碳含量，不等同于应用级纯化产品纯度。
CNT 长度和残留金属未单独定量。
压力、甲烷转化率、碳效率、催化剂寿命和连续运行时间未报告。
作者使用 large-scale 表述，但实际实验催化剂装填量为 0.1 g，不能据此确认工业规模。

source_run.source_title: Synthesis of thin-multiwalled carbon nanotubes by Fe-Mo/MgO catalyst using sol-gel method
source_run.authors_or_assignee: Prashant Dubey; Sang Kyu Choi; Bawl Kim; Cheol Jin Lee
source_run.run_summary: Fe-Mo/MgO citrate precursor decomposed in air at 700 °C; methane CCVD at 900 °C produced a non-uniform mixture with low yield and TGA carbon content.
source_run.notes: Retained as a negative/control condition; combo_key is calculated in the workbook.

source_run.source_title: Synthesis of thin-multiwalled carbon nanotubes by Fe-Mo/MgO catalyst using sol-gel method
source_run.authors_or_assignee: Prashant Dubey; Sang Kyu Choi; Bawl Kim; Cheol Jin Lee
source_run.run_summary: Fe-Mo/MgO citrate precursor decomposed in Ar at 700 °C; methane CCVD at 900 °C produced uniform t-MWCNTs with 452% weight-gain yield and 85% TGA carbon content.
source_run.notes: Best-performing run in this paper; combo_key is calculated in the workbook.

source_run.source_title: Synthesis of thin-multiwalled carbon nanotubes by Fe-Mo/MgO catalyst using sol-gel method
source_run.authors_or_assignee: Prashant Dubey; Sang Kyu Choi; Bawl Kim; Cheol Jin Lee
source_run.run_summary: Fe-Mo/MgO citrate precursor decomposed in H2 at 700 °C; methane CCVD at 900 °C produced uniform t-MWCNTs with 370% weight-gain yield and 82% TGA carbon content.
source_run.notes: Useful comparison to Ar decomposition; combo_key is calculated in the workbook.

catalyst_system.acid_or_complexing_summary: Citric acid complexing; metal ion:citric acid = 1:1.2; solution pH about 1.0
catalyst_system.acid_treatment_purpose: complex metal ions; prevent Mo precipitation; promote homogeneous gel
catalyst_system.calcination_condition: citrate precursor thermally decomposed in air at 700 °C for 2 h
catalyst_system.activation_summary: ground to fine powder before thermal decomposition
catalyst_system.phase_or_state_summary: separate MgMoO4/MgMo2O7/MoO3 phases observed; Fe mainly oxidized
catalyst_system.dispersion_summary: non-uniform distribution and agglomerated catalyst particles
catalyst_system.expected_CNT_type_bias: mixed thick CNTs, carbon fibers and some t-MWCNTs
catalyst_system.catalyst_assessment: [review_assessment] poor control catalyst; low surface area and phase separation correlate with low yield
catalyst_system.evidence_text: Air decomposition produced low BET area, large pores and non-uniform Fe-Mo dispersion; Table 1 reports 60 m²/g.
catalyst_system.evidence_location: PDF pp. 2-5; Section 2.1; Section 3.2; Table 1; Figs. 1-3
catalyst_system.notes: Bracketed MgO-only support values from Table 1 were not entered as catalyst values.

catalyst_system.acid_or_complexing_summary: Citric acid complexing; metal ion:citric acid = 1:1.2; solution pH about 1.0
catalyst_system.acid_treatment_purpose: complex metal ions; prevent Mo precipitation; promote homogeneous gel
catalyst_system.calcination_condition: citrate precursor thermally decomposed in Ar at 500 sccm and 700 °C for 2 h
catalyst_system.activation_summary: ground to fine powder before thermal decomposition
catalyst_system.phase_or_state_summary: no separate Fe2O3 or MoOx phase detected; Fe-Mo nanoclusters dispersed in MgO
catalyst_system.dispersion_summary: well-dispersed 3-6 nm Fe-Mo particles; Mo-rich clusters
catalyst_system.catalyst_assessment: [review_assessment] best route in this paper; high BET area and fine pores correlate with highest yield and uniform product
catalyst_system.evidence_text: Ar decomposition produced 3-6 nm dispersed particles and 202 m²/g BET area; Table 1 and TEM support the assessment.
catalyst_system.evidence_location: PDF pp. 2-5; Section 2.1; Section 3.2; Table 1; Figs. 1-3
catalyst_system.notes: TEM-EDS spot ratio was Mo-rich, but it was not treated as the bulk catalyst composition.

catalyst_system.acid_or_complexing_summary: Citric acid complexing; metal ion:citric acid = 1:1.2; solution pH about 1.0
catalyst_system.acid_treatment_purpose: complex metal ions; prevent Mo precipitation; promote homogeneous gel
catalyst_system.calcination_condition: citrate precursor thermally decomposed in H2 at 500 sccm and 700 °C for 2 h
catalyst_system.activation_summary: ground to fine powder before thermal decomposition
catalyst_system.phase_or_state_summary: Fe-Mo nanoclusters dispersed in MgO; Mo2C phase observed under reducing decomposition
catalyst_system.dispersion_summary: well-dispersed 3-6 nm Fe-Mo particles; Mo-rich clusters
catalyst_system.catalyst_assessment: [review_assessment] strong comparison route; similar dispersion to SG-2 but lower product yield and slightly larger tubes
catalyst_system.evidence_text: H2 decomposition produced 3-6 nm dispersed particles and 210 m²/g BET area; Mo2C was also observed.
catalyst_system.evidence_location: PDF pp. 2-5; Section 2.1; Section 3.2; Table 1; Figs. 1-3
catalyst_system.notes: TEM-EDS spot ratio was Mo-rich, but it was not treated as the bulk catalyst composition.

reactor_process_gas.reactor_type: quartz tube reactor in tube furnace
reactor_process_gas.process_note: Heating rate and time were not reported.
reactor_process_gas.evidence_text: Catalyst was heated to 900 °C under Ar at 1000 sccm.

reactor_process_gas.reactor_type: quartz tube reactor in tube furnace
reactor_process_gas.process_note: The authors describe this step as catalyst pretreatment.
reactor_process_gas.evidence_text: At 900 °C the catalyst was pretreated for 20 min in Ar/H2 at 800/200 sccm.

reactor_process_gas.reactor_type: quartz tube reactor in tube furnace
reactor_process_gas.temperature_effect_summary: Only 900 °C was tested in the reported growth comparison; no temperature window was established.
reactor_process_gas.process_note: CH4 volume fraction is calculated as 1000/2200; all gas flows are reported values.
reactor_process_gas.evidence_text: CNT growth used Ar/CH4/H2 at 1000/1000/200 sccm and 900 °C for 30 min.

yield_quality.yield_calculation_method: reported Eq. (1); w_cat is catalyst before growth and w_tot is catalyst plus carbon after growth
yield_quality.yield_unit_standardized: % carbon weight gain relative to catalyst
yield_quality.yield_standardization_note: Reported value transcribed without conversion to purified CNT mass.
yield_quality.CNT_type_evidence: SEM and TEM show thick CNTs, carbon fibers, narrow CNTs and a mixed t-MWCNT/MWCNT product.
yield_quality.SWCNT_evidence_summary: No SWCNT assignment; multi-wall structures and carbon fibers are visible by TEM/HRTEM.
yield_quality.morphology: non-uniform mixture of thick CNTs, carbon fibers and some t-MWCNTs
yield_quality.evidence_text: Table 1 reports 73% weight-gain yield and 43% TGA carbon; microscopy confirms a mixed, non-uniform product.
yield_quality.notes: 43% is TGA carbon content, not separately quantified residual-metal or application-grade purity. Diameter and length distributions were not reported.

yield_quality.yield_calculation_method: reported Eq. (1); w_cat is catalyst before growth and w_tot is catalyst plus carbon after growth
yield_quality.yield_unit_standardized: % carbon weight gain relative to catalyst
yield_quality.yield_standardization_note: Reported value transcribed without conversion to purified CNT mass.
yield_quality.CNT_type_evidence: TEM/HRTEM shows resolved 4-7 graphene walls and uniform 4-8 nm outer diameters.
yield_quality.SWCNT_evidence_summary: HRTEM wall count confirms multi-walled rather than single-walled tubes.
yield_quality.morphology: uniform, entangled t-MWCNTs; isolated tubes and bundles; clean surfaces
yield_quality.amorphous_carbon_level: low; slight surface deposits noted by HRTEM
yield_quality.evidence_text: Table 1 reports 452% weight-gain yield and 85% TGA carbon; HRTEM gives 4-8 nm diameter and 4-7 walls.
yield_quality.notes: More than 95% of measured tubes were in the 4-8 nm range; length and residual metal were not reported.

yield_quality.yield_calculation_method: reported Eq. (1); w_cat is catalyst before growth and w_tot is catalyst plus carbon after growth
yield_quality.yield_unit_standardized: % carbon weight gain relative to catalyst
yield_quality.yield_standardization_note: Reported value transcribed without conversion to purified CNT mass.
yield_quality.CNT_type_evidence: TEM/HRTEM shows uniform 4-9 nm tubes with 5-8 graphene walls.
yield_quality.SWCNT_evidence_summary: HRTEM wall count confirms multi-walled rather than single-walled tubes.
yield_quality.morphology: uniform, entangled t-MWCNTs with clean surfaces
yield_quality.evidence_text: Table 1 reports 370% weight-gain yield and 82% TGA carbon; HRTEM gives 4-9 nm diameter and 5-8 walls.
yield_quality.notes: Length and residual metal were not reported.

cost_scale_review.catalyst_cost_signal: [review_assessment] high Mo loading may affect catalyst cost; no prices reported
cost_scale_review.purification_cost_signal: [review_assessment] low TGA carbon content and mixed fibers imply a high purification/separation burden
cost_scale_review.scale_signal_reported: Authors describe a large-scale reactor (~100 mm i.d.); actual run used 0.1 g catalyst.
cost_scale_review.scale_up_issue: Low yield and non-uniform product; throughput, lifetime, conversion and continuous operation not reported.
cost_scale_review.safety_risk: [review_assessment] CH4/H2 at 900 °C; combustible gas and high-temperature operation
cost_scale_review.major_cost_driver: [review_assessment] Ar/H2 consumption, 900 °C heating, Mo content and purification burden
cost_scale_review.recommended_next_action: Retain as control when reproducing SG-2; do not prioritize as a production route.
cost_scale_review.missing_critical_fields: pressure; CH4 conversion; carbon efficiency; catalyst lifetime; continuous operation; actual throughput; purification mass balance; residual metal
cost_scale_review.review_note: [review_assessment] Valuable mainly for showing why air decomposition is unfavorable.
cost_scale_review.evidence_text: Air-prepared SG-1 has the lowest BET area, 73% yield, 43% TGA carbon and mixed carbon morphology.

cost_scale_review.catalyst_cost_signal: [review_assessment] high Mo loading may affect catalyst cost; no prices reported
cost_scale_review.purification_cost_signal: [review_assessment] 85% TGA carbon is promising, but MgO/Fe-Mo removal and application-grade purity were not evaluated
cost_scale_review.scale_signal_reported: Authors describe large-scale synthesis and a ~100 mm i.d. reactor; actual run used 0.1 g catalyst.
cost_scale_review.scale_up_issue: Gas utilization, heat/mass transfer, catalyst lifetime, pressure drop and continuous operation not reported.
cost_scale_review.safety_risk: [review_assessment] CH4/H2 at 900 °C; combustible gas and high-temperature operation
cost_scale_review.major_cost_driver: [review_assessment] Ar/H2 consumption, 900 °C heating, Mo content and downstream catalyst/support removal
cost_scale_review.reported_best_condition_summary: Fe:Mo:MgO = 1:1.5:10; citrate sol-gel; Ar decomposition at 700 °C for 2 h; CH4 growth at 900 °C for 30 min
cost_scale_review.reviewed_suitable_condition: Prioritize SG-2 as the first reproduction candidate, while measuring gas conversion and purification burden.
cost_scale_review.recommended_next_action: Reproduce SG-2; verify 452% weight-gain yield, carbon selectivity, residual Fe/Mo/MgO, batch repeatability and lower Ar/H2 demand.
cost_scale_review.missing_critical_fields: pressure; CH4 conversion; carbon efficiency; catalyst lifetime; continuous operation; actual throughput; purification mass balance; residual metal; CNT length
cost_scale_review.review_note: [review_assessment] Strong paper-level candidate, but industrial suitability remains unproven because conversion, lifetime and purification data are absent.
cost_scale_review.evidence_text: SG-2 is the best reported run: 202 m²/g catalyst, 452% yield, 85% TGA carbon and uniform 4-8 nm t-MWCNTs.

cost_scale_review.catalyst_cost_signal: [review_assessment] high Mo loading may affect catalyst cost; no prices reported
cost_scale_review.purification_cost_signal: [review_assessment] 82% TGA carbon is promising, but MgO/Fe-Mo removal and application-grade purity were not evaluated
cost_scale_review.scale_signal_reported: Authors describe a large-scale reactor (~100 mm i.d.); actual run used 0.1 g catalyst.
cost_scale_review.scale_up_issue: H2 catalyst decomposition adds hazard and gas demand; lifetime, conversion and continuous operation not reported.
cost_scale_review.safety_risk: [review_assessment] H2 decomposition at 700 °C plus CH4/H2 growth at 900 °C
cost_scale_review.major_cost_driver: [review_assessment] H2/Ar consumption, 900 °C heating, Mo content and downstream catalyst/support removal
cost_scale_review.reported_best_condition_summary: Fe:Mo:MgO = 1:1.5:10; citrate sol-gel; H2 decomposition at 700 °C for 2 h; CH4 growth at 900 °C for 30 min
cost_scale_review.reviewed_suitable_condition: Use as a direct comparison to SG-2 to determine whether H2 decomposition offers any reproducible benefit.
cost_scale_review.recommended_next_action: Compare SG-3 with SG-2 under identical batches; test whether the extra H2 preparation step is justified by product consistency.
cost_scale_review.missing_critical_fields: pressure; CH4 conversion; carbon efficiency; catalyst lifetime; continuous operation; actual throughput; purification mass balance; residual metal; CNT length
cost_scale_review.review_note: [review_assessment] Good-quality product but lower yield, larger mean diameter and lower Raman IG/ID than SG-2.
cost_scale_review.evidence_text: SG-3 gives 210 m²/g catalyst, 370% yield, 82% TGA carbon and uniform 4-9 nm t-MWCNTs.

evidence_index.value: Synthesis of thin-multiwalled carbon nanotubes by Fe-Mo/MgO catalyst using sol-gel method; DOI 10.5714/CL.2012.13.2.099
evidence_index.evidence_summary: Title page provides article identity, authors, journal, year and DOI.
The authors identify this composition as the best in their composition study.

evidence_index.value: citrate sol-gel; citric acid ratio 1:1.2; pH about 1; dry 120 °C 5 h
evidence_index.evidence_summary: Preparation and complexing details are explicitly described.

evidence_index.value: air / Ar 500 sccm / H2 500 sccm at 700 °C for 2 h
evidence_index.evidence_summary: These three atmospheres define SG-1, SG-2 and SG-3.

evidence_index.value: 0.1 g catalyst; 900 °C; Ar/H2 pretreatment 800/200 sccm for 20 min; Ar/CH4/H2 1000/1000/200 sccm for 30 min
evidence_index.evidence_summary: The same methane CCVD program is used for all three catalysts.
Table 1 reports the two metrics and their bases.
SG-2 has the highest reported product yield.
SG-3 is intermediate between SG-1 and SG-2.
SEM and TEM show a non-uniform mixed product.
More than 95% of measured tubes fall in the reported range.
HRTEM supports a uniform t-MWCNT assignment.
Figure labels report IG/ID for each catalyst product.
The paper explicitly notes that microscopy was performed without purification.

source_level_observations: The paper compares catalyst precursor decomposition atmosphere, not CNT growth temperature.
Ar and H2 decomposition improve Fe-Mo dispersion relative to air; Ar gives the best combined yield and product quality.
High Mo content is presented as beneficial for Fe shielding and methane aromatization, but no lower-Mo run table is provided in this paper.
valuable_unmapped_information: TEM-EDS spot atomic ratios differ from the nominal bulk Fe:Mo ratio and were retained only in catalyst notes.
XPS binding-energy details and catalyst oxidation states are mechanistically valuable but not expanded into dedicated v1 columns.
